paper_title: Polyploid evolution and Pleistocene glacial cycles: A case study from the alpine primrose Primula marginata (Primulaceae)

methods.description: Procedure for collecting and preparing plant samples from various populations of P. marginata for flow cytometry analysis to investigate variation in ploidy levels.

steps.description: To investigate variation of ploidy levels in P. marginata, we collected three to eight individuals from each of 17 populations covering its entire distributional range (Table 4, Figure 2A).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect plant individuals from specified populations of P. marginata.
inputs: List of 17 populations of P. marginata
Number of individuals to collect per population (3-8)

steps.description: A total of 100 transplanted individuals were cultivated at the Botanical Garden of the University of Genova
steps.searchable_summary: Transplant and cultivate collected plant individuals.

steps.description: and later used for flow cytometry analyses at the Institute of Systematic Botany of the University of Zurich.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare cultivated plant individuals for flow cytometry analysis.

methods.description: Procedure for collecting and preparing leaf tissue samples from various Primula species and selected outgroups for subsequent cpDNA phylogenetic analysis.

steps.description: To investigate the phylogenetic relationships of P. marginata, we collected and dried leaf tissue in silica gel from several populations of P. marginata, P. latifolia, P. allionii, P. hirsuta, and related species in Primula sect. Auricula. To provide a broader phylogenetic context for the relationships of P. marginata, we also sampled 16 accessions from 15 species of sect. Auricula for the cpDNA analyses. Additionally, three taxa were selected for outgroup rooting based on published phylogenetic studies of Primula [29,73]: P. cuneifolia (sect. Cuneifolia) and P. parryi and P. cusickiana (sect. Parryi). Therefore, a total of 47 accessions were used for cpDNA analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect leaf tissue samples from specified Primula species, related species, and outgroups for cpDNA analysis.
inputs: List of Primula species (P. marginata, P. latifolia, P. allionii, P. hirsuta, related species in sect. Auricula)
List of outgroup taxa (P. cuneifolia, P. parryi, P. cusickiana)

steps.description: collected and dried leaf tissue in silica gel
steps.searchable_summary: Dry collected leaf tissue samples using silica gel.

steps.description: Samples used in all analyses and GenBank/EBI accession numbers are listed in Table 4, Additional file 1: Tables S2, Additional file 1: Table S3,
steps.searchable_summary: Record and list sample information and GenBank/EBI accession numbers for cpDNA samples.
outputs: Tables listing samples and accession numbers (Table 4, Additional file 1: Tables S2, Additional file 1: Table S3)

methods.description: Procedure for selecting and preparing plant samples from specific Primula species for subsequent nrDNA phylogenetic analysis.

steps.description: A total of 15 accessions belonging to selected populations of P. marginata, P. allionii and P. latifolia were used for nrDNA analysis (Table 4).
steps.searchable_summary: Select accessions from specified Primula species for nrDNA analysis.
inputs: List of Primula species (P. marginata, P. allionii, P. latifolia)

steps.description: Samples used in all analyses and GenBank/EBI accession numbers are listed in Table 4, Additional file 1: Tables S2, Additional file 1: Table S3,
steps.searchable_summary: Record and list sample information and GenBank/EBI accession numbers for nrDNA samples.
outputs: Tables listing samples and accession numbers (Table 4, Additional file 1: Tables S2, Additional file 1: Table S3)
